fields.description:
## Why
Two repo-hygiene items from the 2026-07-08 audit OSS finding #6 cleanup bucket:
1. `.joe-janitor/` internal agent-tooling reports are committed to the public mirror (`tools.md` and `report-*.md` are tracked) — internal-process noise for outsiders. Decision: remove the tracked reports and ignore the directory **locally only** (`.git/info/exclude`, NOT the shared `.gitignore`), so the tooling keeps working on this machine without advertising the path in the public tree.
2. `.tokensave/` (code-graph MCP cache) is untracked but unignored — it pollutes `git status` on any machine running the tokensave server. Add it to the shared `.gitignore` alongside `.serena/` (both per-developer tool caches, so a shared ignore is correct here).

## Descoped (item 3, api-stability version): NOT NEEDED
The audit's third item ("docs/api-stability.md hardcodes a stale version") is **already remediated** by ticket 9f40 (doc-drift sweep D2): `tests/unit/test_version_sync.py` is a CI gate that asserts the `currently X.Y.Z` marker in docs/api-stability.md equals `pyproject.toml`'s version and fails the build on drift. The marker is already correct (0.7.1) and can never go stale again. Removing the literal would BREAK that guard (the test hard-fails if the marker is absent). So there is nothing to do here; item 3 is dropped.

## What / files
- `git rm --cached` the tracked `.joe-janitor/` files; add `.joe-janitor/` to `.git/info/exclude` (NOT `.gitignore`)
- `.gitignore`: add `.tokensave/`; remove the duplicated `dist/` + `build/` entries (they appear twice — audit low finding)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `git ls-files` shows nothing under `.joe-janitor/`; the directory persists on disk, excluded via `.git/info/exclude` only
- [ ] `.tokensave/` is ignored by the shared `.gitignore`; `git status` is clean while the tokensave server is active
- [ ] `.gitignore` no longer duplicates `dist/` / `build/`
- [ ] `make lint` green (no code churn)
